instructions.content: Run `skill-auditor evaluate <domain>/<skill-name> --json --store` to score a skill on the 9-dimension framework.
Use `skill-auditor batch <skill1> <skill2> --store` to audit multiple skills in one pass.
Build the auditor binary before first use: `bun run build:skill-auditor`.
Interpret scores using grade bands: A ≥126/140, B+ 119-125, B 112-118, C+ <112.
Skills scoring below C+ (112) are blocked from registry publication.
D1 Knowledge Delta must score ≥15/20; scores below 15 indicate the skill teaches nothing beyond LLM baseline.
D9 Eval Validation requires scenario subdirectories (scenario-N/) each containing task.md, criteria.json, and capability.txt.
criteria.json must be a weighted_checklist with items summing exactly to 100.
NEVER recommend cross-domain skill aggregation (e.g. do not merge terraform + github-actions skills).
NEVER skip baseline comparison in recurring audits — without a prior audit.json, score regressions go undetected.
Detect duplication using similarity thresholds: <20% monitor, 20-35% plan aggregation, >35% immediate action.
Generate remediation plans with T-shirt sizing (S/M/L/XL) and measurable score delta estimates.
Anti-pattern entries must include NEVER/WHY/BAD/GOOD structure for D3 scoring.
The References section must be the last H2 and use a pipe-delimited table with Topic, Reference, and When to Use columns.
ALWAYS use the Navigation Hub pattern for skills that consolidate multiple related capabilities.
NEVER apply subjective scoring without deterministic checks — subjective scores drift between evaluators and cannot be automated in CI.
original_snippets: manually scoring without running the binary

instructions.content: NEVER create kitchen-sink skills covering multiple unrelated tasks — broad scope kills D7 pattern recognition and makes skills hard to trigger.
original_snippets: combining terraform and github-actions in one skill
